completion_boundary: This registry proves an 18-layer canonical architecture and evidence mapping. It does not prove every layer is connected, deployed or end-to-end verified.

layers.name: Experience and Omnichannel Interface
layers.purpose: Command Centre, Jarvis Pop, web, mobile, desktop, text, voice and accessible novice-first interaction surfaces.

layers.name: Gateway, API and Edge
layers.purpose: REST, MCP, webhook, voice and event ingress with schema validation, rate limits and safe routing.

layers.name: Identity, Consent and Tenant Boundary
layers.purpose: Authentication, authorization, RBAC, consent, tenant isolation and data-scope enforcement.

layers.name: Sovereign Governance and Approval Control Plane
layers.purpose: Constitution, policy, approval, budget, autonomy, kill-switch and owner-authority enforcement.

layers.name: Agent Orchestration and Role Council
layers.purpose: Planner, builder, verifier, risk and release role separation with bounded agent lifecycle and task delegation.

layers.name: Skill, Tool and Connector Fabric
layers.purpose: Governed Skills, MCP tools, adapters, connector lifecycle, capability discovery and sandboxed integration.

layers.name: Model Routing and Inference
layers.purpose: Deterministic-first, local/free-first model selection, provider preflight, parallel panels and cost-aware escalation.

layers.name: Workflow, Event, Queue and Scheduler
layers.purpose: Dependency graphs, durable queues, idempotency, retries, scheduling, dead letters, replay and compensation.

layers.name: Domain Application Services
layers.purpose: Isolated Health, Business, XRP/HBAR, Property, CFO, VTI, agency, SaaS and future domain services.

layers.name: Transactional Data and State
layers.purpose: SQLite, PostgreSQL, cache, queue state, object storage, schemas, migrations, retention and tenant isolation.

layers.name: Memory and Knowledge Fabric
layers.purpose: Working, episodic, semantic, procedural, project and audit memory with lexical, vector, graph and citation retrieval.
open_gaps: Permission-filtered graph and vector retrieval proof

layers.name: Evidence, Provenance and Audit
layers.purpose: Hashes, source lineage, append-only audit, requirement traceability, decisions, conflicts, evidence packs and proof states.

layers.name: Security, Privacy and Supply Chain
layers.purpose: Zero trust, secrets, least privilege, prompt-injection defence, dependency provenance, scanning, privacy and data classification.

layers.name: Observability, Cost and Operations
layers.purpose: Logs, metrics, traces, alerts, SLOs, model/tool spend, usage ceilings, incident response and operator controls.

layers.name: Verification, Simulation and Release Gates
layers.purpose: Unit, contract, integration, E2E, security, policy, accessibility, load, digital-twin, canary and release verification.

layers.name: Infrastructure, Deployment and Resilience
layers.purpose: Local, cloud and hybrid deployment, environment isolation, backups, restore, disaster recovery and service-specific rollback.
open_gaps: Restore and DR proof in connected staging

layers.name: Continuity, Versioning and Rollback
layers.purpose: Git history, state snapshots, changelogs, continuity packs, deprecation, restore points, resume prompts and rollback evidence.

layers.name: Source Reconstruction and Continuous Learning
layers.purpose: Archives, chat exports, OCR, transcription, source scouting, delta ingestion, contradiction tracking and governed improvement loops.
open_gaps: Connected hybrid index and governed learning promotion

cross_cutting_requirements: Every layer must define ownership, contracts, permissions, data classes, tests, evidence, rollback and runtime state.
Shared repository does not imply shared runtime or shared confidential data.
No layer may self-certify production readiness.
All live, financial, destructive, publication, credential and clinical/device actions remain approval-gated.
